obligations.obligation:
CENTRAL ELECTRICITY REGULATORY COMMISSION
NEW DELHI
Petition No. 498/TT/2025
Coram:
Shri Ramesh Babu V., Member
Shri Harish Dudani, Member
Date of Order: 15.01.2026
In the matter of:
Approval under Section 62 read with Section 79(1)(d) of the Electricity Act, 2003 and
under Regulation 15(1)(a) and R...

The Petitioner has made the following prayers in the instant Petition:
a) “Approve the trued-up Transmission Tariff for 2019-24 block and transmission
tariff for 2024-29 block for the assets covered under this petition, as per para 20
& 21 above.

e) Allow the petitioner to recover the shortfall or refund the excess Annual Fixed
Charges, on account of Return on Equity due to change in applicable Minimum
Alternate/Corporate Income Tax rate as per the Income Tax Act, 1961 (as
amended from time to time) of the respective financial year directly ...

f) Approve the reimbursement of expenditure by the beneficiaries towards petition
filing fee, and expenditure on publishing of notices in newspapers in terms of
Regulation 94 (1) Central Electricity Regulatory Commission (Terms and
Conditions of Tariff) Regulations, 2024, and other expenditure (if a...

g) Allow the petitioner to bill and recover Licensee fee and RLDC fees & charges,
separately from the respondents in terms of Regulation 94 (3) and (4) Central
Electricity Regulatory Commission (Terms and Conditions of Tariff) Regulations,
2024.

i) Allow the petitioner to file a separate petition before the Commission for claiming
the overall security expenses and consequential IOWC on that security expenses
as mentioned at para 27 above.

j) Allow the petitioner to file a separate petition before the Commission for claiming
the overall insurance expenses and consequential IOWC on that insurance
expenses as mentioned at para 27 above.

k) Allow the petitioner to file a separate petition before the Commission for claiming
the overall capital spares at the end of tariff block as per actual as mentioned at
Para 27 above.

l) Allow the petitioner to claim expenses of CTUIL borne by POWERGRID through
a separate petition as mentioned at para 28 above.

imposed by any
statutory/Govt./municipal authorities shall be allowed to be recovered from the
beneficiaries
and pass such other relief as the Commission deems fit and appropriate under the
circumstances of the case and in the interest of justice.”
Background
3.

The Petitioner has served a copy of the Petition on the Respondents, and notice
regarding the filing of this Petition has been published in the newspapers in accordance
with Section 64 of the Electricity Act, 2003 (hereinafter referred to as “the Act”).

The capital cost of the transmission assets has been calculated in accordance
with Regulation 19(1) and 19(3) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations.

However,
the Petitioner is at liberty to provide all necessary documents at the time of truing up of
tariff in support of delay of 242 days and the claim for time over-run with reference to
Asset-III shall be considered on merit.”
Page 14 of 90
Order in Petition No. 498/TT/202520.

The capital cost of the transmission assets has been calculated in accordance
with Regulation 19 of the 2019 Tariff Regulations.

The details of Initial Spares allowed as per the revised Plant and Machinery Cost,
in accordance with Regulation 13 of the 2014 Tariff Regulations & Regulation 23 of the
2019 Tariff Regulation, are as follows:
(₹ in lakh)
Initial Excess
Plant &
Norms as Spares Initial
Machinery
per worked out Spares...

The Petitioner in the instant true-up Petition has claimed the following ACE for
the 2019-24 tariff period with respect to Asset-1, Asset-2, Asset-3, and Asset-4 based
on actual expenditure in accordance with Regulation 25(1)(d) of the 2019 Tariff
Regulations as per the following details:
(₹ in lakh...

Furthermore, the Petitioner vide affidavit dated 29.11.2024 has also submitted
the Liability Flow Statement containing the head-wise and party-wise details of the
actual ACE incurred during the 2019-24 tariff period for the respective transmission
assets under Regulation 24(1)(a), 24(1)(b) and 25(1)...

Accordingly, the ACE claimed by the Petitioner under Regulation 24(1)(a), 24(1)(b) and
25(1)(d) of the 2019 Tariff Regulations for the 2019-24 tariff period is hereby allowed,
and the details are as follows:
(₹ in lakh)
Asset-1
Actual ACE
Particulars Regulations 2021- 2022- 2023-
2019-20 2020-21
22 ...

The Weighted Average Rate
of Depreciation (WAROD) has been worked out for Asset-1, Asset-2, Asset-3 and
Asset-4 as per the rates of depreciation specified in Regulation 33 of the 2019 Tariff
Regulations.

The IoL has
been calculated based on the actual interest rate submitted by the Petitioner in
accordance with Regulation 32 of the 2019 Tariff Regulations.
